title: Your Eyes Deceive You
editorialSummary: Optical illusions grouped by the perceptual mechanism that causes them. Ambiguous figure illusions (figure-ground or multistable perception): Rubin's Vase, Necker Cube, Duck-Rabbit, Old-Young Woman. Geometric distortion illusions (lines or shapes appear different sizes or angles than they are): Muller-Lyer, Ponzo, Cafe Wall, Zollner. Motion illusions (static images perceived as moving): Rotating Snakes, Fraser Spiral, Peripheral Drift, Pinna-Brelstaff. Colour/contrast illusions (identical colours or brightness perceived as different): Checker Shadow, Hermann Grid, Simultaneous Contrast, Mach Bands. False trail: the Necker Cube involves perceived motion (rotation) but is canonically an ambiguous figure illusion; Cafe Wall involves both colour contrast and geometric distortion but is primarily classified as geometric.
